jokes.text: Happy! Come here, Happy!
jokes.setup: Calling child by name
jokes.punchline: Young Burns was literally named 'Happy' - ultimate ironic contrast
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character development - Mr. Burns was literally named Happy, making his current state perfectly ironic

jokes.text: would you rather live with this twisted, loveless billionaire?
jokes.setup: Parent offering child a choice of guardians
jokes.punchline: Describes the billionaire as 'twisted, loveless' to the child's face
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about abandoning a child, with blunt honesty about the adoptive parent

jokes.text: Let's roll.
jokes.setup: Child choosing between parents and billionaire
jokes.punchline: Immediately chooses money with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Burns's lifelong greed established early with casual acceptance of abandonment

jokes.text: Oh, well. At least we still have his little brother, George.
jokes.setup: Mother just lost her son to a billionaire
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions replacement child like he's interchangeable
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about parental indifference, treating children as replaceable

jokes.text: Trust me. It'll be funny when I'm an old man.
jokes.setup: Character singing old song
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to acknowledge the joke for the audience
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's anachronistic humor - Burns acknowledging his old-fashioned references

jokes.text: Bobo? I, uh, meant Lobo. Uh, Sheriff Lobo. They never should have canceled that show.
jokes.setup: Burns caught saying mysterious name 'Bobo'
jokes.punchline: Covers with reference to obscure TV show
jokes.explanation: Burns's embarrassed cover-up with reference to actual cancelled 1970s TV show

jokes.text: I won't get what I really want. - No one does.
jokes.setup: Burns lamenting he won't get what he wants for birthday
jokes.punchline: Smithers's matter-of-fact existential despair
jokes.explanation: Smithers delivering philosophical darkness with casual acceptance

jokes.text: Lobo! Lobo! Bring back Sheriff Lobo!
jokes.setup: Burns trying to cover his Bobo obsession
jokes.punchline: Escalates into passionate plea for cancelled TV show
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the cover-up into absurd passion for obscure show

jokes.text: Where is that dreaded pinata? Ow! Ow! Ow! Missed me. Heh-heh! Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer as human pinata getting hit
jokes.punchline: Taunts crowd while still being hit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer physical comedy - too dumb to stop taunting while being injured

jokes.text: Not funny. And the one in my pants really hurts.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about being human pinata
jokes.punchline: TMI about injury in inappropriate area
jokes.explanation: Low-brow anatomical humor - functional but not clever

jokes.text: I must harness his fractured take on modern life.
jokes.setup: Burns wanting Homer to perform comedy
jokes.punchline: Pretentious description of Homer's stupidity as artistic
jokes.explanation: Burns's pompous misinterpretation of Homer's incompetence as avant-garde comedy

jokes.text: I'll get him started on some snappy Sinbad-esque material.
jokes.setup: Smithers offering to help Homer with comedy
jokes.punchline: References Sinbad as the height of comedy
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference joke - Sinbad representing safe, bland 1990s comedy

jokes.text: Is 'poo poo' one word or two?
jokes.setup: Homer writing comedy material
jokes.punchline: Focused on juvenile bathroom humor logistics
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish approach to comedy perfectly in character, though predictable

jokes.text: Actually, Mom, a tweaking of Mr. Burns's foibles, if done with the greatest of care... could earn Dad a special place in the old man's heart.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving sophisticated comedy advice
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old using overly intellectual language about roasting
jokes.explanation: Lisa's precocious vocabulary contrasted with the lowbrow context

jokes.text: I paint a frowny face on my butt and pull down my pants!
jokes.setup: Homer describing his Burns impression
jokes.punchline: Reveals crude physical comedy plan
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor perfectly fitting Homer's character, but low craftsmanship

jokes.text: Does either of you know what 'incontinent' means?
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart laughing at word 'incontinent'
jokes.punchline: Lisa revealing they don't understand what they're laughing at
jokes.explanation: Lisa as voice of reason exposing the ignorance behind the laughter

jokes.text: Hey, Flanders! You smell like manure! Uh-oh. Better cancel that dinner party tonight. Thanks for the nose news, neighbor!
jokes.setup: Homer insulting Flanders about his smell
jokes.punchline: Flanders taking it as helpful information and being grateful
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Flanders's relentless positivity vs Homer's meanness

jokes.text: Hey! No one-termers. Get away from me, loser.
jokes.setup: Politicians mingling at Burns's party
jokes.punchline: One-term presidents being rejected by other politicians
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about electoral success as social currency

jokes.text: I'd just like to say this gig sucks! Hey, up yours, Springfield!
jokes.setup: Ramones introduced as entertainment for Burns
jokes.punchline: Punk band immediately insulting the audience and venue
jokes.explanation: Perfect character authenticity - Ramones staying true to punk attitude even at fancy party

jokes.text: Go to hell, you old bastard. Hey, I think they liked us.
jokes.setup: Ramones finish insulting birthday song
jokes.punchline: Misreading the hostile crowd as appreciation
jokes.explanation: Punk band's oblivious misreading of a hostile crowd

jokes.text: Have the Rolling Stones killed. Oh, sir, those aren't-- Do as I say!
jokes.setup: Burns angry at the performance
jokes.punchline: Orders murder of wrong band, refuses correction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Burns's petty vindictiveness and confusion, plus his refusal to be corrected

jokes.text: I have some sad news to report. A small puppy, not unlike Lassie, was just run over in the parking lot. Now it's time for the comedy stylings of Homer Simpson!
jokes.setup: Smithers announces tragic news about puppy death
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to comedy introduction
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of inappropriate timing and emotional whiplash

jokes.text: Are you ready to laugh? Oh, that poor dog. I said, 'Are you ready to laugh?' Quiet, you awful man.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get audience pumped after dog death news
jokes.punchline: Audience focused on tragedy, Homer getting increasingly desperate
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Homer's obliviousness to reading the room

jokes.text: You know, Mr. Burns is so cheap-- What? I ju-- I mean, you know, Mr. Burns is so old-- How dare you!
jokes.setup: Homer starting roast jokes about Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns taking offense, Homer making it worse with each attempt
jokes.explanation: Escalating disaster as Homer digs himself deeper with each insult attempt

jokes.text: Here's an impression of Mr. Burns that you might find a little cheeky. I'm Mr. Burns. Blah, blah, blah. Do this, do that. Blah, blah, blah. I think I'm so big. Blah, blah, blah!
jokes.setup: Homer promising cheeky Burns impression
jokes.punchline: Terrible impression combined with exposed butt
jokes.explanation: Homer's juvenile approach culminating in literal butt-showing - crude but character-appropriate

jokes.text: I'll never wiggle my bare butt in public again. I'd like to believe that this time. I really would.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to reform after disaster
jokes.punchline: Marge's weary skepticism based on history
jokes.explanation: Perfect marriage dynamic - Marge's loving but realistic view of Homer's promises

jokes.text: She'll be ready for your Aunt Selma's birthday. I knew it.
jokes.setup: Homer promising his butt won't appear in public again
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning next butt performance for family event
jokes.explanation: Immediate contradiction of his promise, proving Marge right about his pattern

jokes.text: the only existing nude photo of Mark Twain; and that rare first draft of the Constitution with the word 'suckers' in it.
jokes.setup: Smithers listing Burns's valuable possessions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd historical artifacts
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical artifacts suggesting cynical view of American history

jokes.text: Liar! I'll give you the thrashing of a lifetime!
jokes.setup: Burns denying he wants his teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent reaction proving he does want it
jokes.explanation: Burns's violent overreaction revealing his true feelings

jokes.text: You gotta start selling this for more than a dollar a bag. We lost four more men on this expedition. If you can think of a better way to get ice, I'd like to hear it.
jokes.setup: Apu complaining about ice prices
jokes.punchline: Reveals deadly ice expedition treated casually
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - treating deadly ice harvesting as mundane business

jokes.text: Hey, Apu, this bag of ice has a head in it. Ooh, a head bag! Those are chock-full of... heady goodness.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about head in ice bag
jokes.punchline: Apu's terrible pun about heads while treating it casually
jokes.explanation: Apu's characteristic bad pun combined with casual acceptance of body parts in merchandise

jokes.text: Hey, it's a teddy bear! Ew, gross. It's probably diseased or something. Here, Maggie.
jokes.setup: Bart finding valuable teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Dismisses it as gross and gives to baby sister
jokes.explanation: Ironic moment - most valuable bear in world treated as garbage by child

jokes.text: Enough! Stop this grotesque charade. Now, find my teddy. Oh, and, uh, leave the costume.
jokes.setup: Burns rejecting Smithers's teddy bear performance
jokes.punchline: Still wants to keep the costume despite calling it grotesque
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about Burns's desires combined with his contradictory behavior

jokes.text: Maggie, I'm trying to watch TV. Put that moldy, old bear down. Moldy? Old? I'm gonna get something to eat!
jokes.setup: Homer casually dismissing bear while watching TV about it
jokes.punchline: Completely missing that he has the exact bear being discussed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - the bear he wants is right in front of him but food wins

jokes.text: It's supposed to be... doing a little dance. No. Bear want to live.
jokes.setup: Robotic teddy bear malfunctioning
jokes.punchline: Robot expressing existential desire for survival
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about artificial intelligence gaining consciousness and immediately fearing death

jokes.text: You stupid stair!
jokes.setup: Homer falling down stairs
jokes.punchline: Blaming the inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer move - refusing to take responsibility, blaming objects

jokes.text: Well, Burns isn't getting this back cheap. I can tell you that. He's gonna have to give me... my own recording studio.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to negotiate with Burns
jokes.punchline: Completely unrealistic demand for recording studio
jokes.explanation: Homer's grandiose and impractical dreams when he thinks he has leverage

jokes.text: Homer,you're drooling on the mike again.
jokes.setup: Homer in recording studio fantasy
jokes.punchline: Even in fantasy, he's incompetent and disgusting
jokes.explanation: Even in his own fantasy, Homer can't escape his gross habits

jokes.text: I think we should just give him the bear that he cherishes so much.
jokes.setup: Family discussing what to do with valuable bear
jokes.punchline: Marge suggesting kindness while family laughs at the idea
jokes.explanation: Marge as moral center being dismissed by her greedy family

jokes.text: No! Let's send Burns the eye in the mail. He'll pay more money if he thinks the bear's in danger.
jokes.setup: Family planning to extort Burns
jokes.punchline: Child suggesting psychological torture tactics
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of child casually suggesting kidnapper tactics

jokes.text: Well, why can't I be greedy once in a while?
jokes.setup: Marge being convinced to go along with extortion
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Marge embracing selfishness
jokes.explanation: Character development - even saint-like Marge succumbs to greed occasionally

jokes.text: Naturally, I can't pay you much of a reward, because I'm strapped for cash. As you can see, this old place is falling apart.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to be poor
jokes.punchline: While standing in his mansion (implied visual contradiction)
jokes.explanation: Burns's transparent attempt at poor-mouthing despite obvious wealth

jokes.text: Reject the first offer. Reject the first offer. May I offer you a drink? Sorry, Burns. No deal!
jokes.setup: Homer rehearsing negotiation strategy
jokes.punchline: Immediately misapplies advice to drink offer instead of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - following advice literally and inappropriately

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! I gotta call Burns! I gotta call Burns! Maybe I can still get that drink.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about making deal
jokes.punchline: Still fixated on the rejected drink offer
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities remain hilariously skewed toward immediate gratification

jokes.text: A million dollars and three Hawaiian islands-- good ones, not the leper ones. Done! Now give me that bear!
jokes.setup: Homer making outrageous demands
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately agreeing to impossible terms
jokes.explanation: Escalation showing Burns's desperation and Homer's casual reference to leper colonies

jokes.text: Bad baby! Bad Maggie! Give me the bear. The bear. The bear!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get bear from Maggie
jokes.punchline: Escalating desperation and repetition while fighting baby
jokes.explanation: Homer's increasing desperation creating uncomfortable comedy as he fights his baby

jokes.text: The deal's off, Mr. Burns. The bear stays here.
jokes.setup: Family greed vs Marge's protective instincts
jokes.punchline: Marge choosing baby's happiness over million dollars
jokes.explanation: Marge's moral center asserting itself - choosing family over fortune

jokes.text: Son! You gotta help me! I hit three people on the way over here... and I don't have any insurance! So, how's by you?
jokes.setup: Grandpa arriving in crisis
jokes.punchline: Casual greeting after admitting to hit-and-run accidents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grandpa Simpson moment - treating catastrophic crime casually

jokes.text: Sixty-four slices of American cheese. Sixty-four. Sixty-three. Two. One.
jokes.setup: Homer eating cheese slices one by one
jokes.punchline: Counting down entire package obsessively through the night
jokes.explanation: Iconic absurdist sequence showing Homer's compulsive relationship with food

jokes.text: Have you been up all night eating cheese? I think I'm blind.
jokes.setup: Marge discovering Homer's cheese binge
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact report of temporary blindness
jokes.explanation: Logical conclusion of Homer's cheese obsession with typical underreaction to consequences

jokes.text: Excuse me. We wanted to see the geek... who valued the happiness of his children more than money. Right here. Aw, you said his head was the size of a baseball.
jokes.setup: People coming to see Homer as curiosity
jokes.punchline: Disappointed he's not physically deformed, just morally unusual
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary - decent behavior treated as freakish spectacle

jokes.text: After lunch, can I whip you? Nope. Aw, no fair.
jokes.setup: Homer being punished at work
jokes.punchline: Coworker disappointed he can't participate in torture
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor but relatively standard for the show's style

jokes.text: See, Maggie? See the fun box? No! My box! My box!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to trade box for teddy bear
jokes.punchline: Maggie choosing worthless box over valuable bear
jokes.explanation: Baby's preference for simple box over million-dollar bear showing pure innocence

jokes.text: Two plus two is four. Two plus two is four. I can see why this is so popular.
jokes.setup: Burns watching basic educational TV
jokes.punchline: Finding simple math fascinating entertainment
jokes.explanation: Burns's disconnect from normal entertainment showing his otherworldly perspective

jokes.text: Give me my bear, Simpson! It's The Sou-ou-oul Mass Transit System.
jokes.setup: Burns demanding bear urgently
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by terrible foreign TV programming
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist interruption showing Burns's control of all television

jokes.text: What's that you say? You can live without television... as long as you have beer? Wrong. All beer trucks heading toward Springfield have been diverted.
jokes.setup: Burns controlling TV to punish Homer
jokes.punchline: Escalates to controlling all beer supply
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Burns's power and pettiness - controlling entire town's vices

jokes.text: This town will be as dry as a bone. And if the rest of you beer-swilling tube jockeys out there... have a problem with this, talk to Homer Simpson.
jokes.setup: Burns controlling all TV channels
jokes.punchline: Cutting off beer supply and turning entire town against Homer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Burns's power and manipulation, turning town into weapon

jokes.text: My old man can't get a beer because his old man won't give a bear to another old man. Let's get him! Wait! Why are we gettin' him? Look, fellas. The first snapdragon of the season. Never mind! Let's just get him!
jokes.setup: Angry mob planning to attack Homer
jokes.punchline: Completely distracted by flower, then remembering to be angry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mob psychology satire - easily distracted from violence by beauty, then returning to it

jokes.text: Look, fellas. The first snapdragon of the season. Never mind! Let's just get him!
jokes.setup: Angry mob ready to attack Homer
jokes.punchline: Momentarily distracted by pretty flower before returning to violence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how easily distracted even angry mobs can be

jokes.text: Smithers, I'm home! What? Already? Yes. Is it my imagination, or is TV getting worse?
jokes.setup: Burns watching TV he controls
jokes.punchline: Complaining about quality of programming he mandated
jokes.explanation: Burns's lack of self-awareness about his role in TV quality decline

jokes.text: What have we become? We've given the word 'mob' a bad name.
jokes.setup: Mob realizing they've been terrible to child
jokes.punchline: Self-aware complaint about mob reputation
jokes.explanation: Self-referential joke about mob behavior with perfect ironic awareness

jokes.text: So, good sand today, hmm? Well, just this once. Eww! Damn you paparazzo!
jokes.setup: Burns awkwardly trying to connect with baby
jokes.punchline: Eating sand, then blaming photographer for documenting it
jokes.explanation: Burns's desperate attempt to relate to baby resulting in sand consumption

jokes.text: Beaten by an infant. What could be more humiliating? What a scoop!
jokes.setup: Burns lamenting defeat by baby
jokes.punchline: Photographer excited about documenting his humiliation
jokes.explanation: Burns's humiliation compounded by media attention

jokes.text: From now on I'm only going to be good and kind to everyone! I'm sorry, sir. I don't have a pencil. Ah, don't worry. I'm sure I'll remember it.
jokes.setup: Burns declaring he'll be good forever
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing the promise when he can't write it down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - momentary goodness immediately forgotten

jokes.text: Marge, I'm confused. Is this a happy ending or a sad ending? It's an ending. That's enough.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about episode's emotional resolution
jokes.punchline: Marge's pragmatic dismissal of narrative satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on storytelling with perfect character voices

jokes.text: Bobo, I know I say this every century, but I'll never leave you behind again.
jokes.setup: Burns reunited with bear in future
jokes.punchline: Admits he's made this promise repeatedly over centuries
jokes.explanation: Callback showing Burns's eternal pattern of losing and finding bear
